methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Reality Defender, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Ben Colman and Gaurav Bharaj, has raised $50M to date. Valuation analysis draws on two methodologies triangulated against a peer set of five same-stage, same-sector comparables: Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M).

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, anchoring to the $50M total raised as a proxy baseline. This yields an implied valuation of $95M. Peer-median analysis identifies a $5.0B median across the five-company comp set, with valuations ranging from $500M floor to $32.0B ceiling. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, market position, and investor composition within frontier AI.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $81M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects meaningful uncertainty around Reality Defender's positioning within the frontier AI landscape. Stage-progression assumes linear funding progression; peer-median incorporates market repricing of early-stage AI equity. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to comparables, technology differentiation, and whether the comp set's valuations reflect sustainable multiples or speculative froth in the sector.
